title: Sharp mixing-time bounds for random walks on expander Cayley graphs
abstract: We obtain sharp upper and lower bounds on the total-variation mixing time of simple random walks on Cayley graphs of expander families.
ai_proof_review:
## Setup

Classical mixing-time theory plus a new sharp lower bound based on a return-time argument.

## Upper bound (Section 3)

Standard spectral argument. Clean.

## Lower bound (Section 4)

More delicate. Uses a coupling that holds with high probability, with the failure mode controlled by a hitting-time tail estimate (Lemma 4.5). The tail estimate cites a long preprint of one of the authors; I could not independently verify the cited bound but the citation appears legitimate and the application is standard.

## Overall

The result is plausible and the methods are mainstream. Mild conditional confidence due to the external lemma; if that lemma holds (very likely), the paper is fine.
